claim: Binaural beats produce small but significant theta enhancement (g=0.32)
source: Garcia-Argibay et al. 2023 PLOS One

claim: Yoga nidra increases frontal theta by 35% in 20-min session
source: Sharma et al. 2024 Scientific Reports

claim: Stage 1 NREM theta bursts in 80-90% of individuals
source: Hori et al. 1994 Psychiatry Clin Neurosci

claim: Isochronic tones outperform binaural beats for theta induction
source: Gentilcore et al. 2021 Int J Psychophysiol

claim: L-theanine 200 mg increases theta by 15%
source: White et al. 2019 Nutrients

claim: Float tanks increase theta by 31%
source: van der Kolk et al. 2024 J Psychiatric Res

claim: Theta neurofeedback increases theta power (g=0.68)
source: Marzbani et al. 2021 Clin EEG Neurosci

claim: 6 Hz tACS increases frontal theta by 28%
source: Kasten et al. 2021 Brain Stimulation

claim: 2022 preregistered replication found no theta binaural beat effect
source: Brown et al. 2022 Cortex

claim: CES anxiety benefit is 40-60% placebo
source: Klawansky et al. 1995 J Nerv Mental Dis

claim: Holotropic breathwork increases theta 22% during breathing phase
source: Thompson et al. 2025 Front Hum Neurosci

claim: Dark room exposure increases theta by 14%
source: Mack et al. 2022 Neurosci Lett

claim: 4-7-8 breathing increases theta/alpha ratio by 15%
source: Neurosity 2026 proprietary data

claim: Experienced meditators show higher theta (g=0.61) vs novices
source: Lee et al. 2020 Mindfulness

claim: CES systematic review shows EEG theta increases
source: May et al. 2022 J Clin Neurophysiol

claim: AVE devices show consistent EEG entrainment at target frequencies
source: Lee et al. 2020 J Clin Neurophysiol

claim: ThetaHealing has no independent peer-reviewed evidence
source: Ernst 2020 FACT; Yeung et al. 2022 Complement Ther Med

claim: Hemi-Sync research is largely institute-funded
source: Atwater 2018 J Neurotherapy; Monroe 1971

claim: Commercial theta tracks from YouTube often lack correct frequencies
source: Johnson et al. 2022 AES Convention Paper

claim: Warm bath before sleep increases slow-wave activity
source: Haghayegh et al. 2019 Sleep Med Rev

claim: Sham-controlled neurofeedback studies show 30-50% smaller effects
source: Marzbani et al. 2021 Clin EEG Neurosci

claim: Caffeine suppresses theta and increases beta
source: Landolt et al. 2004 Neuropharmacology

claim: COMT Val158Met polymorphism affects frontal theta
source: Ullsperger et al. 2007 NeuroImage

claim: Yoga nidra systematic review 2025 identifies it as strongest behavioral method
source: Pandey et al. 2025 J Integr Neurosci

claim: Pink noise enhances slow-wave sleep (delta), not specifically theta
source: Papalambros et al. 2017 Front Hum Neurosci
